_note: PURLs are pinned to the versions Trivy detects in this image so a future rebuild past the fix invalidates the statement and forces re-review. Current pins: docker/docker@v28.5.2 (vendored as library code by the docker-buildx 0.35.0 and docker-compose 5.3.1 cli-plugins, installed from their upstream GitHub releases), containerd/v2@v2.2.4 (vendored by dockerd 29.6.0 and docker-buildx 0.35.0 — the standalone containerd.io package is 2.2.5 and scans clean), and Go stdlib@v1.26.4 (the toolchain all five Docker binaries are currently built with; Go 1.26.5 fixes CVE-2026-39822 but no Docker binary has been rebuilt on it yet). When apt resolves newer docker/containerd, the plugin ARGs in docker/runner/Dockerfile are bumped, or upstream rebuilds on a patched Go, this file must be reviewed: if the new versions are past the fixes the entries can be removed; otherwise the PURLs must be updated. NOTE: statements here are per-CVE analyses (dead code, unreachable plugin paths, or no privilege boundary in this deployment) — never blanket package ignores. Live reachable paths with no such analysis stay visible until upstream ships a fixed build.
author: runs-fleet maintainers

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-34040
vulnerability.description: Docker authorization bypass via plugin disabling. Affects dockerd authorization handler.

statements.impact_statement: Present in the docker-buildx 0.35.0 and docker-compose 5.3.1 cli-plugins as vendored library code only. Both binaries are Docker API clients; they do not run dockerd or its authorization handler. The vulnerable code path is dead code in the client binaries. The actual daemon (docker-ce) on this image is at 29.6.0 which contains the fix.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-41567
vulnerability.description: PUT /containers/{id}/archive executes container binary on the host. Daemon-side archive handler.

statements.impact_statement: Vendored in the docker-buildx 0.35.0 and docker-compose 5.3.1 cli-plugin client binaries. Neither binary serves the /containers/{id}/archive endpoint; only dockerd does. Vulnerable handler code is unreachable from these binaries.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-42306
vulnerability.description: Race condition in docker cp allows bind mount redirection to host. Daemon-side cp handler.

statements.impact_statement: Vendored in the docker-buildx 0.35.0 and docker-compose 5.3.1 cli-plugin client binaries. The race condition is in the daemon's cp implementation. Plugins talk to the daemon over the API; they do not execute the vulnerable cp handler themselves.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-53488
vulnerability.description: containerd CRI plugin propagates image-config LABEL instructions to containers without sanitization, enabling arbitrary host command execution via a crafted container image.

statements.impact_statement: Vulnerable code is in containerd's CRI plugin (image LABEL handling). Trivy flags the vendored containerd/v2 v2.2.4 library inside dockerd 29.6.0 and the docker-buildx 0.35.0 client plugin. This image runs containers via dockerd over the legacy moby socket; containerd's CRI plugin is never loaded and no CRI image-pull/LABEL path is exercised. The buildx plugin is a pure API client that does not host the runtime. The standalone containerd.io runtime on this image is 2.2.5, which contains the fix. PURL pinned so a plugin/daemon rebuild past the fix invalidates this statement.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-53489
vulnerability.description: containerd CRI plugin does not validate symlinked container log paths during checkpoint restore, enabling arbitrary host file read.

statements.impact_statement: Vulnerable code is in containerd's CRI plugin checkpoint/restore path (container-log symlink validation). Trivy flags the vendored containerd/v2 v2.2.4 library inside dockerd 29.6.0 and the docker-buildx 0.35.0 client plugin. This image runs `docker run` via the legacy moby socket; the CRI plugin is never loaded and CRI checkpoint/restore is never invoked. The buildx plugin is an API client that does not host the runtime. The standalone containerd.io runtime is 2.2.5 (fixed). PURL pinned so a rebuild past the fix invalidates this statement.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-53492
vulnerability.description: containerd CRI plugin trusts CDI (Container Device Interface) annotations from untrusted checkpoint image metadata without validation, allowing device and host mount injection.

statements.impact_statement: Vulnerable code is in containerd's CRI plugin CDI annotation handling during checkpoint restore. Trivy flags the vendored containerd/v2 v2.2.4 library inside dockerd 29.6.0 and the docker-buildx 0.35.0 client plugin. This image runs `docker run` via the legacy moby socket; the CRI plugin is never loaded, no checkpoint/restore occurs, and CDI device injection is never exercised. The buildx plugin is an API client that does not host the runtime. The standalone containerd.io runtime is 2.2.5 (fixed). PURL pinned so a rebuild past the fix invalidates this statement.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-39822
vulnerability.description: os: Root escape via symlink plus trailing slash. os.Root.Open/Create/OpenFile follow a final-component symlink outside the Root when the path ends in '/'. Fixed in Go 1.25.12 / 1.26.5 (GO-2026-4970).

statements.impact_statement: Trivy flags the Go 1.26.4 stdlib in five Docker binaries (dockerd 29.6.0, docker, docker-proxy, docker-buildx 0.35.0, docker-compose 5.3.1); none has been rebuilt on a fixed Go yet — the fix shipped days ago. govulncheck -mode=binary symbol analysis shows the vulnerable os.Root symbols are absent from docker, docker-proxy, and docker-compose (dead code). dockerd and docker-buildx do contain os.Root usage, but on these runner hosts the only untrusted principal is workflow code, which by design already holds full Docker-socket access — root-equivalent on a single-tenant, ephemeral, self-terminating instance — so the escape crosses no privilege boundary the deployment doesn't already concede. PURL pinned to stdlib@v1.26.4: when Docker republishes on Go 1.26.5+ the detected version changes, this statement stops matching, and the fixed binaries scan clean.
